Culinary Innovation

The Art of Vegan Cheese Crafting

Published on May 15, 2026•By Chef Akanksha

Cheese is the soul of Italian cuisine. When I set out to specialize in plant-based Italian cooking, the biggest challenge wasn't the pasta, but the cheese. How could you replicate the stretch of fresh mozzarella or the deep umami of aged Parmigiano-Reggiano without dairy?

Most commercial vegan cheeses rely on starches and oils. They melt, but they lack soul. I wanted to treat plant-based cheese-making like a traditional craft.

By leveraging raw cashews, almonds, and homemade rejuvelac (a fermented grain liquid), I began aging cheeses in controlled settings. The result was remarkable: we developed lactic acidity, complex rind structures, and a natural melt that rivals traditional cheeses.

In this blog, we explore the step-by-step fermentation science behind cashew-based mozzarella and how small-batch plant cheese can elevate restaurant menus.

Chef Akanksha

Creating food that connects tradition with compassion.
